body:
Hey Emeka,

So the hydration reminder hit at literally the worst moment — I was mid-sentence explaining thesis structure to a student at the Writing Center and my phone buzzed three times in a row. Appreciated the persistence, less appreciated the timing.

That said — bottle is filled. 32 oz, cold, sitting next to me now. Between two class blocks back-to-back this morning and a student gov meeting tonight with Amara, I genuinely forget until my head starts feeling off. The reminder cadence you set up is doing more work than I'd like to admit.

One ask: can we shift the afternoon ping to around 3 PM instead of 2? That's usually when I'm transitioning between the Writing Center shift and prep for evening obligations. Better window to actually act on it.

Thanks —
Brian

Brian,

Glad the bottle got filled — that's the whole job. And noted on the timing clash; 3 PM works better for your schedule, I've updated it.

For what it's worth, the pattern I'm seeing is that you reliably hydrate in the morning and then drop off once the afternoon block starts. Makes sense given how your days are structured in Syracuse — class, tutoring, meetings, basically no natural pause. The reminders are just forcing the pause you're not building in yourself.

A couple of things that might help:
- Keep a second bottle wherever you do your longest sit (Writing Center desk, student gov room)
- Set the reminder label to something specific so it doesn't read like noise — "Water, not coffee" tends to work for people who ignore generic pings

Let me know if the 3 PM slot actually lands better this week. If not, we adjust again.

Emeka

Hey Emeka,

Following up on the class block situation from last week — still waiting to hear if the conflict got resolved on the registrar's end. My Tuesday and Thursday blocks are packed as-is, and with my Writing Center shift stacked right after, there's zero buffer if something runs over or gets rescheduled without notice.

Amara flagged the same issue on her end, so it's not just me. Figured it was worth looping you in officially since you're the one with system access. Can you check whether the hold on the course section was lifted, or if there's still a flag on my account?

Thanks — quick turnaround appreciated if possible.
Brian

Hi Brian,

Got your message — appreciate you flagging this and looping me in. I pulled up your account and there was indeed a registration hold tied to an outdated departmental prerequisite check. It's been cleared on my end as of this morning, so you should be good to re-attempt enrollment. Give it about 15–20 minutes to propagate through the system before you try.

For Amara, she'll need to submit her own request through the portal since I can't action her account without a direct ticket from her. Tell her to reference case #TK-2247 and it'll move faster.

Let me know if the block doesn't drop or if anything else pops up. Happy to stay on it.

Emeka
Student Support — GreenRider Tech

Emeka,

Just checked — enrollment went through. You're a lifesaver, genuinely. I would've been staring at a scheduling hole going into the fall with no good options left.

I passed the case number to Amara; she's filing her ticket now. She also said, and I quote, "tell him he's the only competent person in that office" — her words, not mine, but I'm inclined to agree.

Alright, I'm heading into my student government meeting in ten. Marcus already texted asking why I look stressed in my calendar photo so apparently even my schedule has a vibe. Will catch you properly at some point when things slow down — which, per usual, is never.

Thanks again.
Brian

Emeka —

Yeah, I know, strange to be submitting a ticket to my own brother. Anyway.

Class block issue: I'm getting a scheduling conflict error when I try to register the Tue/Thu 2:00–3:30 block. System flags a 15-minute overlap with my Writing Center tutoring shift, but the Writing Center coordinator already signed off on an adjusted start time on her end. Need a manual override processed before add/drop closes Friday — I don't have a lot of flex here given everything else stacked in the week.

Let me know what you need from me to move this.

— Brian

Brian,

Not weird. You're a ticket number like everyone else. (Kidding. Mostly.)

Pulled your account — the conflict flag is accurate. Platform auto-locks any schedule overlap without exception, so I can't push through a manual override without written confirmation from your Writing Center coordinator sent directly to support. Once that's in, turnaround is 24–48 hours on my end.

Given that add/drop closes Friday, you want that email from her today or tomorrow at the latest. Subject line should reference your name and the adjusted time.

— Emeka | Academic Scheduling Support

Emeka,

Coordinator confirmation sent — she cc'd me on it, so it should already be in the queue. Subject reads "Writing Center Schedule Adjustment – B. Patterson."

Quick clarification before you process: the override just needs to cover the 2:00–2:15 overlap window, not the full block, right? Want to make sure she worded it correctly so you're not stuck waiting on a revision.

Also flagging: I'm in class blocks most of tomorrow and student gov runs late Wednesday night, so if anything urgent comes up, morning or after 4 is the window.

— B

Brian,

Confirmation received — wording is fine, no revision needed. Override is processed. Tuesday/Thursday 2:00–3:30 should show as registered in your schedule portal within the hour; conflict flag should be cleared. Go check and shoot me a reply if it's still showing anything weird.

You're all set on our end. And tell Marcus I said hey.

— Emeka | Academic Scheduling Support

Hey Emeka,

Following up on my Thursday tutoring shift (2–5 PM) — there's a scheduling conflict I didn't catch until Amara flagged it yesterday. Student gov reliably runs past 3:30, sometimes closer to 4, so that last hour is basically a coin flip between the two.

Can you check whether there's coverage available for the 4–5 slot, or should I loop in the shift coordinator directly? I'm not trying to just ghost the Writing Center desk mid-afternoon.

Also — there may still be a Monday class block conflict sitting in the system from last semester. If so, go ahead and clear it; it's not accurate anymore and I'd rather not have it ghost-blocking anything.

Thanks,
Brian

Hi Brian,

Got you sorted on both fronts. Thursday 4–5 is covered — moved it over to Keisha, she had open availability this week and was actually looking for extra hours, so it works out. The Monday class block from last semester has been cleared too; you're clean in the system.

One thing to note going forward: the coordinator has asked that schedule changes get submitted through the portal at least 48 hours in advance. I handled this one manually since it was short notice, but just flagging so it doesn't catch you off guard next time.

You're all set for the week. Let me know if anything else comes up.

— Emeka
Writing Center Support | GreenRider Tech

Hey Emeka,

Following up on the Gmail API ticket from last week — still getting the 403 on the OAuth scope request. I've got the `gmail.readonly` scope listed in the consent screen but the token refresh is throwing `insufficientPermissions` every time the app tries to pull inbox metadata. Attached the error log below.

I know you said to give it 24 hrs to propagate but it's been closer to five days at this point. Between tutoring shifts and student gov this week my windows to actually debug are tight, so if there's a step I'm missing I'd rather know now than Friday.

— Brian

Brian,

Got your follow-up — sorry for the delay, high ticket volume this sprint.

Quick checklist before we escalate:
- Confirm the OAuth client type is set to **Web application**, not Desktop
- Make sure you've re-issued the token *after* adding the scope (old tokens don't inherit new scopes automatically)
- Check that the test user email is added under the OAuth consent screen → **Test users** (if the app is still in Testing mode, only listed accounts can authorize)

If all three check out and you're still hitting the 403, reply with your Cloud project ID and I'll pull the API audit log on my end. Should be able to turnaround same day.

Emeka
Support | GreenRider Tech

Emeka,

That was it — test user wasn't added. Classic. I'd walked through every scoping doc the Writing Center has on OAuth and somehow skipped the most obvious step. Re-issued the token, inbox metadata is pulling clean now.

One follow-on question: the research project I'm building this for needs to read message headers across ~200 emails per session. Should I be thinking about quota limits at the free tier, or is that well under the daily ceiling?

Appreciate the quick list format, by the way — exactly what I needed between meetings.

— B

Hey Emeka,

Following up on the ticket I opened last week re: Google Calendar API integration. Still getting a 403 on OAuth scope authorization when trying to pull calendar event data into the scheduling tool I built for the Writing Center — students need to book tutoring slots and right now the whole thing just throws errors on auth. I attached the error log to the original ticket (#GCA-88412) but happy to paste it here if that's easier.

For context: I'm trying to read/write events on a shared departmental calendar (not personal), which I suspect is the scope issue. Using `calendar.events` — should I be requesting `calendar` full access instead? My student gov meeting ran long last night so I didn't get to test the fix I was theorizing, but I wanted to ping you before the week gets away from me.

Appreciate any direction you can give.

Brian Patterson
Research Assistant | GreenRider Tech, Syracuse

Hi Brian,

Thanks for the follow-up and for referencing the original ticket — pulled it up. You're right to suspect scope. For shared/organizational calendars (not owned by the authenticating user), `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/calendar.events` is often insufficient if the calendar's sharing settings restrict external write access. You'll want to request `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/calendar` AND ensure the service account (or OAuth client) has been explicitly granted access to that specific shared calendar in Google Workspace admin settings.

Quick checklist:
- Confirm the shared calendar ID (not 'primary')
- In Calendar settings → Share with specific people, add your service account email
- Re-run OAuth consent with the broader scope

If you're using a service account rather than a user OAuth flow, domain-wide delegation may also need to be enabled by your org's admin. Let me know which auth method you're using and I can narrow it down further.

Best,
Emeka Patterson | Support Engineer, Google Workspace APIs

Emeka —

Okay this is actually helpful, thank you. I'm using a service account (not user OAuth), so domain-wide delegation is probably exactly what's missing — GreenRider's IT admin set up the project but I don't think they enabled that. I have a gap between my afternoon class block and my Writing Center shift today, so I'm going to use that window to loop in IT and get delegation configured. Will also double-check the calendar sharing settings; I never explicitly added the service account email there, which, in hindsight, is an obvious miss.

One follow-up: once delegation is enabled, do I need to re-generate the service account key, or does the existing JSON credentials file carry over? Trying to minimize turnaround since the Writing Center goes live with student bookings next Monday.

Also — unrelated but — are you going to mom and dad's in August? Adaeze has been texting me about it like it's a diplomatic summit.

Brian

Brian,

Good news: no need to regenerate the key. The existing service account JSON credentials file remains valid — domain-wide delegation is configured at the Google Workspace Admin console level (Security → API Controls → Domain-wide Delegation), not at the key level. Once your IT admin enables it and scopes it to `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/calendar`, your current credentials will work. Just make sure the code impersonates a user who *has* access to the shared calendar (set the `subject` field in your JWT/credentials to that user's email).

You should be good well before Monday. If auth still fails after the admin changes propagate (can take up to 24 hrs), reply here and I'll escalate.

As for August — tell Adaeze yes, I'm coming, and I don't need a three-week advance itinerary to confirm it. Some things never change.

Good luck with the launch.

Emeka Patterson | Support Engineer, Google Workspace APIs
Ticket #GCA-88412 — marking Resolved pending your confirmation

Hey Emeka,

Following up on the Drive API issue I flagged last week — still running into the same 403 on service account auth when trying to pull files from shared drives. I'm using a JSON key credential and the domain-wide delegation is enabled on our end, but the impersonation call keeps dying at the token exchange step. Attached the stack trace again in case the first one got buried.

Timing is bad on my end (tutoring shift ran late, then student gov ran over — classic), so I haven't had a window to dig into the scopes config myself. But my gut says it's either the subject field in the JWT claim or the API isn't enabled on the correct GCP project. Can you confirm which project ID you have on file for us?

Appreciate the help. Let me know if you need me to regenerate the service account key or adjust permissions on the shared drive side.

Brian

Brian,

Got the trace — yeah, that 403 is a scope mismatch, not the JWT subject. The service account has `drive.readonly` authorized in the OAuth consent screen but the shared drive endpoint requires `drive` (full) or explicitly `drive.file` depending on your access pattern. Domain-wide delegation is fine, that part's configured correctly.

Quick fix: go to Admin Console → Security → API Controls → Domain-wide Delegation, find your service account client ID, and add `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/drive` to the scopes list. Then re-auth the credential in your app — no new key needed. Should resolve it without touching the GCP project config.

Also double-checked: the API is enabled on the right project, so you're good there. If it's still throwing after the scope update, ping me directly and I'll pull the request logs on our end.

Emeka
Support — GreenRider Tech Platform Team

Hey Emeka,

Following up on ticket #4471 from last week — the Notion API integration still isn't pulling database entries into my workspace. Specifically, the GET /v1/databases/{id}/query endpoint keeps returning a 403 even after I regenerated the integration token and re-shared the database. I've triple-checked the permissions on the Notion side.

For context: I'm using this to auto-populate a research tracker for my RA work at GreenRider Tech. Between that, my Writing Center shifts, and student gov meetings stacking up this week, manually copying entries is genuinely not an option right now.

Can you confirm whether the integration token needs to be re-added at the page level vs. the database level? The docs are contradicting each other on this.

— Brian

Brian,

Thanks for the follow-up — sorry for the delay on this one.

You're hitting a known friction point: in Notion's current permission model, sharing a database with an integration does NOT automatically grant access to its parent page hierarchy, and the 403 usually means the integration is missing explicit access to a top-level page above the database. Go to the root page > Share > Invite your integration there. Then re-run the query.

Also double-check that your integration has 'Read content' capability enabled under Settings > Connections — it's off by default on newer integrations.

If that doesn't resolve it, send me your integration ID (not the secret) and I can check on our end.

— Emeka Patterson | Support Team

Emeka,

That was it — parent page access, not the database. Docs genuinely need a callout box for that, it's buried.

Working now. Research tracker is live, which means I can stop copy-pasting during the 15-minute window between my afternoon class block and tutoring shift like some kind of caffeinated Victorian clerk.

Appreciate the quick turnaround once you got to it. You can close #4471.

— B

Hi Support,

Following up on my hydration reminder setup — the 'fill water bottle' notification is either firing mid-lecture or completely missing the mark. My schedule is pretty compressed: two back-to-back class blocks, then a Writing Center tutoring shift, then student gov. The only realistic refill window I've got is around 11:45–12:10 before I have to be at the Writing Center. Can you adjust the trigger to hit that slot instead of whatever it's doing now (2:17 PM, for the record — deeply unhelpful)?

Appreciate it.

— Brian Patterson
GreenRider Tech, Syracuse

Hi Brian —

Wait. Brian *Patterson*? From Syracuse? This is Emeka. Professionally: I've updated your notification window to 11:45 AM on your scheduled active days — change should propagate by end of day. Personally: it is genuinely unhinged that you submitted a support ticket to reach your own brother. You could have texted.

I've also flagged your account for a daily reminder cadence since 'completely missing the mark' suggests you need the assist. Confirmation email incoming.

Drink water,
— Emeka
Customer Support

Emeka —

Confirmed, notification came through at 11:48. Close enough, I'll take it. Water bottle: filled. Threat level: downgraded.

For the record, I didn't know you were on this queue — I submitted the ticket at 7 AM between readings and wasn't exactly cross-referencing the agent roster. Student gov ran long tonight (Amara and I disagreed loudly about discretionary budget allocations, as is tradition), so I'm only seeing your reply now. Anyway. Tell Mom I said hi since apparently this is how we communicate now.

— B

Brian —

Ticket closed on my end. Mom has already been forwarded this thread as proof of life — she says you work too hard and should call more, which, same.

Seriously though: you're running between the Writing Center, class, and student gov with apparently zero margin. Hydration is not a nice-to-have. I've set the reminder to repeat daily; let me know if you want a second prompt at end-of-day too and I'll update the config. No ticket required — just text me like a normal person.

Stay hydrated,
— Emeka

Hey Emeka,

Following up on the ticket I submitted last Thursday — still getting locked out of the course registration portal during my Tuesday/Thursday class block. System keeps throwing a time-conflict error that, as far as I can tell, does not actually exist.

Fast version of the situation:
- Trying to add POLS 340 (T/Th 10–11:15) and ENGL 302 (T/Th 12:30–1:45)
- Error code: CR-4401, "scheduling overlap detected"
- Tried three browsers, cleared cache, used campus WiFi and my hotspot separately — same result every time

My Writing Center shift runs Tuesdays at 3, student gov meeting is Thursday evening, and I'm already working around two back-to-back class blocks to keep any of this functional. A phantom scheduling error is not where I want to spend the margin.

I'm aware you're my brother. I'm filing this through proper channels anyway. Please just be fast.

— Brian

Hi Brian,

Got the ticket — and the footnote about proper channels. Very professional of you.

Here's what was happening: ENGL 302 had a Thursday lab section in the system that was cancelled this semester but never removed from the course metadata. The registration portal read it as overlapping your POLS 340 block and flagged the CR-4401. It's entirely our error — I've manually cleared the conflict flag on your account. You should be able to enroll in both sections now.

Give it another try. If a new error code comes up, reply here with it and I'll escalate directly rather than making you refile.

Also — Tuesday 10 AM to Thursday evening with a Writing Center shift in between sounds like a schedule built by someone who hates himself a little. Drink water.

— Emeka
Student Support, GreenRider Tech

Hey Emeka,

Following up on the class block situation from last week — still waiting to hear if the conflict got resolved on the registrar's end. My Tuesday and Thursday blocks are packed as-is, and with my Writing Center shift stacked right after, there's zero buffer if something runs over or gets rescheduled without notice.

Amara flagged the same issue on her end, so it's not just me. Figured it was worth looping you in officially since you're the one with system access. Can you check whether the hold on the course section was lifted, or if there's still a flag on my account?

Thanks — quick turnaround appreciated if possible.
Brian

Hi Brian,

Got your message — appreciate you flagging this and looping me in. I pulled up your account and there was indeed a registration hold tied to an outdated departmental prerequisite check. It's been cleared on my end as of this morning, so you should be good to re-attempt enrollment. Give it about 15–20 minutes to propagate through the system before you try.

For Amara, she'll need to submit her own request through the portal since I can't action her account without a direct ticket from her. Tell her to reference case #TK-2247 and it'll move faster.

Let me know if the block doesn't drop or if anything else pops up. Happy to stay on it.

Emeka
Student Support — GreenRider Tech

Emeka,

Just checked — enrollment went through. You're a lifesaver, genuinely. I would've been staring at a scheduling hole going into the fall with no good options left.

I passed the case number to Amara; she's filing her ticket now. She also said, and I quote, "tell him he's the only competent person in that office" — her words, not mine, but I'm inclined to agree.

Alright, I'm heading into my student government meeting in ten. Marcus already texted asking why I look stressed in my calendar photo so apparently even my schedule has a vibe. Will catch you properly at some point when things slow down — which, per usual, is never.

Thanks again.
Brian

Hi Emeka,

Following up on the Writing Center scheduling issue from last Tuesday. My 2–4 PM shift still isn't showing as logged in the system — I covered three back-to-back sessions (two argumentative essays, one lit review) and none of it is reflected in my hours. I've got enough going on between class blocks and student gov prep that chasing phantom timesheets is not really in the budget.

Can you check on the backend whether the shift was imported correctly? My supervisor said it might be a sync error on the portal side. Let me know what you need from me to fix it.

Thanks,
Brian

Hey Brian,

Got your message — yeah, looks like there was a batch import failure on our end for shifts logged between 6/17 and 6/21. You're not the only one caught in it, so don't stress. I can manually enter the hours if you send me the session log (date, time, student ID or initials, session type). The standard form works too if your supervisor already signed off on a paper copy.

Should have it corrected within 24–48 hours once I have what I need. Ping me if it's urgent and I'll bump it up the queue.

— Emeka

Emeka,

Attaching the session log now — three entries, all from the 2 PM block on 6/18. Supervisor signed the paper copy the same day, so that's accounted for. I'll also note that two of those sessions ran over by about 15 minutes each (students were working on APA citations, which, as you can imagine, is its own special kind of purgatory). Not sure if the overage needs to be flagged separately or just rounds to the hour.

Also heads up: I'm in student government meetings most of Thursday, so if you need anything else from me, earlier in the day or after 6 PM works best. Priya can also vouch for my whereabouts if the system demands a character witness.

Brian

Brian,

Got the attachment — hours are entered and pending supervisor approval, should clear by end of week. For the overage: our system rounds to the nearest 15 minutes, so you're good, no separate flag needed. Just make sure your supervisor initials the paper copy in the overage column if your center tracks it locally.

Also noted re: Thursday. I'll close this ticket once the approval goes through and email you confirmation. If it's not resolved by Friday morning, hit me back and I'll escalate.

And yeah, APA citations are genuinely one of the worst things we've done to students. Godspeed.

— Emeka
